title: Shri Jagannath Temple, Puri
intro: The Shri Jagannath Temple, located in the city of Puri, Odisha, is one of the most famous and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Jagannath.
description:
Shri Jagannath Temple – Puri, Odisha
Introduction
The Shri Jagannath Temple, located in the city of Puri, Odisha, is one of the most famous and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Jagannath. This temple is considered one of the primary pilgrimage sites in Hinduism and is an essential part of the Char Dham Yatra.
In this temple, Lord Jagannath is worshipped along with his brother, Lord Balabhadra, and sister, Devi Subhadra. The temple is famous worldwide for the grand annual Rath Yatra (Chariot Festival).
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Jagannath Temple
 Other Names: Jagannath Dham, Puri Jagannath Temple
 Location: Grand Road, Puri, Odisha 752001, India
 Presiding Deities: Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Devi Subhadra
 Significance: One of the Char Dhams
 Nearest Major City: Bhubaneswar (approx. 60 km)
History and Significance
The Shri Jagannath Temple is one of India's most ancient and significant religious sites. It is dedicated to Lord Jagannath, a special form of Lord Vishnu.
According to religious beliefs, Lord Jagannath resides in Puri Dham to bestow his grace upon his devotees. The temple’s annual Rath Yatra is world-famous, where Lord Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra travel through the city on massive chariots, joined by millions of devotees.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar: Distance from the temple is approx. 60 km. Taxi and bus services are available from Bhubaneswar Airport to Puri.
🚆 By Train
 Puri Railway Station: Distance from the temple is approx. 2 km. Auto-rickshaws, taxis, and local transport are easily available from the railway station to the temple.
🛣️ By Road
Puri is well-connected by road to major cities like Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, and Kolkata. You can reach here by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
The Puri Jagannath Temple remains open for devotees throughout the year.
Best time for the Yatra:
October to February, as the weather is more pleasant during this period.
Special Occasions:
 Rath Yatra
 Snana Purnima
 Chandan Yatra
Temple Timings
🕒 5:00 AM to 11:00 PM
(Timings may change during festivals and special religious programs.)
Main Festivals
 Rath Yatra: In this world-famous festival, Lord Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra travel on huge chariots, giving darshan to the devotees.
 Snana Purnima: A special bathing festival of Lord Jagannath, Balabhadra, and Subhadra is organized on this day.
 Chandan Yatra: A special worship and religious festival of the Lord is organized.
Nearby Attractions
 Puri Beach: A famous beach located on the coast of the Bay of Bengal.
 Gundicha Temple: An important religious site associated with the Rath Yatra.
 Narendra Tank: A famous holy water body in Puri.
 Konark Sun Temple: A world-famous historical temple located about 35 km from Puri.
Dress Code
For temple visits:
 It is advised to wear modest and respectful clothing.
 Traditional attire is considered appropriate in accordance with the religious environment.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Prasad facilities
 Help centers
 Nearby hotels and Dharmashalas (guesthouses)
 Local transport facilities
 Security arrangements
Conclusion
The Shri Jagannath Temple in Puri is one of the holiest religious sites in India. The devotion to Lord Jagannath, the grand tradition of the Rath Yatra, and the spiritual significance of the temple make it famous worldwide. A pilgrimage to Puri Dham is considered a sacred journey filled with faith, devotion, and a profound spiritual experience for the devotees.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Jagannath Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Devi Subhadra.
faq.q2: Where is the Jagannath Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Puri, Odisha.
faq.q3: Of which pilgrimage is the Jagannath Temple a part?
faq.a3: It is a major Dham of India's Char Dham Yatra.
faq.q4: Which is the most famous festival of the Jagannath Temple?
faq.a4: Rath Yatra is the most famous festival of this temple.
faq.q5: Which is the nearest railway station to the Puri Jagannath Temple?
